key_risks: July 2 jobs report could surprise and trigger a sharp rotation reversing recent sector leadership before the exit close
Biotech and regional banks carry high volatility; recent strength may mean-revert given stretched up-day shares
A relief rally in mega-cap tech/Nasdaq would cause this defensive/cyclical tilt to underperform the SPX benchmark
Sticky inflation (PCE 4.1%, PPI 6.5%) could pressure rate-sensitive financials and banks if yields spike

portfolio.rationale: Defensive sector with strongest recent relative strength (+7.17% vs SPX 30d), low beta 0.40, supported by rotation into defensives amid Nasdaq weakness.
Leading momentum with +7.83% 7d and +15.71% vs SPX 30d on broad strength; high up-day share supports continuation, though volatile.
Manufacturing PMI 49-month high (55.7) supports cyclicals; +7.81% vs SPX 30d with manageable volatility and at 52w high.
Strong relative strength (+8.90% vs SPX 30d) and steepening curve dynamics favor banks; high up-day share signals breadth.
Defensive quality tilt with positive 7d return and low beta 0.33, provides ballast against tech-led drawdown into jobs report.

portfolio_rationale: Tilt toward defensive and cyclical-value leaders showing relative strength while tech/growth corrects; underweight high-beta Nasdaq exposure ahead of July 2 jobs report which adds event risk into the exit close.
rationale_summary: Rotate into healthcare, industrials, biotech, banks, and dividends that are outperforming during the Nasdaq drawdown, balancing defensive low-beta names with PMI-supported cyclicals.
